name: Szechuan Pork and Broccoli W/Adaptable Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork into narrow 1-inch strips, about 1/4 inch thick. Set aside.
2. Cut green onions, bell pepper, broccoli, and onion into 1-inch pieces. Set aside.
3. In a bowl, combine garlic, ginger, red pepper flakes, hot water, sugar, catsup, and soy sauce.
4. Heat peanut or vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
5. Stir-fry the pork in oil until browned; remove from skillet and keep warm.
6. Add more oil if needed, then stir-fry all vegetables (broccoli, green onions, bell pepper, onion) until tender crisp.
7. Return the pork to the skillet and add the sauce mixture; cook until thickened.
8. Serve with hot boiled rice. Yield is 8 servings.
Diabetic exchange: One serving equals 1 meat, 2 vegetables, 2 fats; also 228 calories, 666 mg sodium, 50 mg cholesterol, 11 gm carbohydrate, 10 gm protein, 17 gm fat.
